overall_summary: ElizaOS is transitioning into a high-velocity beta phase, focusing on expanding infrastructure to WhatsApp and Gmail while managing critical UI/UX bugs on the ElizaCloud platform. Community sentiment is currently mixed due to token migration confusion and technical friction in the new dashboard.
key_facts: Babylon platform access opened to the top 5,000 users on the waitlist to address delivery timeline concerns.
ElizaCloud dashboard currently experiences broken mousepad scrolling on the API explorer and other sub-pages.
The 'Send request' feature on ElizaCloud returns an 'api key is required' error despite keys being present in the UI.
ElizaOS finalized major integrations for WhatsApp, Gmail, and the N8N workflow engine in the core repository.
The SAID Protocol was introduced to provide agents with verifiable on-chain digital identities on Solana.
ElizaOS received a golden checkmark verification on February 19, marking a project milestone.
A major database overhaul was initiated in the core repository to improve long-term system speed and reliability.
Developers reported a 100% success rate in detecting two Solana rugpulls (DARKTRUMP and NVIDIA) two hours before they crashed.
open_questions: Is there still a live Eliza agent that can answer questions about the most recent ElizaOS codebase?
Does 'use a different key' mean users can enter their own API keys (like OpenAI key for gpt-4o) similar to OpenRouter BYOK?
For Milady, we have BNB and Solana - are both valid?
How many users still need to migrate?
Is there any way to migrate ai16z after missing the deadline?
Why is Ai16z coin pumping if the new migrated coin is ElizaOS?

new_issues_prs.title: feat: add SAID Protocol on-chain Solana identity for ElizaOS agents
new_issues_prs.author: kaiclawd
new_issues_prs.significance: Introduces a digital passport system for agents to perform secure, verifiable on-chain actions.

new_issues_prs.title: refactor(core): strict typing for logger runtime
new_issues_prs.author: Fankouzu
new_issues_prs.significance: Improves code quality and type safety by removing 'any' types in the core logger module.

new_issues_prs.title: [Bug] URL in message triggers duplicate LLM calls
new_issues_prs.author: thewoweffect
new_issues_prs.significance: Critical efficiency issue causing doubled token costs and duplicated responses when URLs are present.

overall_focus.claim: The project is undergoing a massive architectural shift toward v2.0.0, supporting multi-language runtimes in Rust, Python, and TypeScript while stripping non-essential infrastructure.

discord_updates.summary: Focused on ElizaCloud bug reporting, specifically broken scrolling and API explorer failures.
Debates regarding Shaw's multi-platform communication strategy (Twitter for AI/Dev, Farcaster for Crypto) and token value concerns.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users are requesting per-model pricing transparency and a formal pricing breakdown in the ElizaCloud dashboard.
Significant confusion remains regarding the difference between $ai16z and $elizaos token contracts.

strategic_insights.insight: By integrating WhatsApp and Gmail, ElizaOS is pivoting from a crypto-native tool to a general-purpose productivity framework.
implications_or_questions: How will the team handle the increased security implications of Gmail/WhatsApp access?

strategic_insights.insight: The success of the Solana rugpull detection system suggests a high-value niche for ElizaOS agents in real-time security auditing.
implications_or_questions: Should this be integrated as a core 'security plugin' for the ecosystem?

market_analysis.observation: Community sentiment reflects a 'building year' mindset for 2026, with recovery tied to the launch of the Jeju network.
market_analysis.relevance: Manages investor expectations regarding short-term token performance.
